# C++ Testing with GoogleMock

Nitro generates a `Mock${ClassName}` GoogleMock stub for every `NativeImpl.cpp`
module. This lets you unit-test your C++ bridge wrapper without a real device.

---

## Prerequisites

GoogleTest and GoogleMock must be available. The simplest setup via CMake's `FetchContent`:

```cmake
# In your test CMakeLists.txt
include(FetchContent)
FetchContent_Declare(
  googletest
  URL https://github.com/google/googletest/archive/refs/tags/v1.14.0.tar.gz
)
FetchContent_MakeAvailable(googletest)
```

---

## Generated files

`nitrogen generate` produces two test-only files for C++ modules:

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `lib/src/generated/cpp/test/my_module.mock.g.h` | GoogleMock `MockMyModule` class |
| `lib/src/generated/cpp/test/my_module.test.g.cpp` | Starter main + smoke test |

Example generated mock header:

```cpp
// my_module.mock.g.h — Generated by Nitrogen. Do not edit.
#pragma once
#include <gmock/gmock.h>
#include "my_module.native.g.h"   // abstract HybridMyModule

class MockMyModule : public HybridMyModule {
public:
    MOCK_METHOD(double, compute, (double a, double b), (override));
    MOCK_METHOD(std::string, describe, (const Point& p), (override));
    MOCK_METHOD(Status, getStatus, (), (override));
    // Stream emit helpers delegate to the generated implementation
    using HybridMyModule::emit_onStatusChanged;
};
```

---

## Writing tests

```cpp
// my_module_test.cpp
#include <gtest/gtest.h>
#include "my_module.mock.g.h"
#include "my_module.bridge.g.h"   // generated C bridge declarations

// Register the mock as the active implementation before each test.
class MyModuleTest : public ::testing::Test {
protected:
    MockMyModule mock;

    void SetUp() override {
        my_module_register_impl(&mock);
    }
    void TearDown() override {
        my_module_register_impl(nullptr);
    }
};

TEST_F(MyModuleTest, ComputeCallsImpl) {
    EXPECT_CALL(mock, compute(3.0, 4.0))
        .WillOnce(testing::Return(7.0));

    // Call through the generated C bridge (same path Dart uses)
    double result = my_module_compute(3.0, 4.0);
    EXPECT_DOUBLE_EQ(result, 7.0);
}

TEST_F(MyModuleTest, ComputeMultipleCalls) {
    EXPECT_CALL(mock, compute(testing::_, testing::_))
        .Times(3)
        .WillRepeatedly([](double a, double b) { return a + b; });

    EXPECT_DOUBLE_EQ(my_module_compute(1.0, 2.0), 3.0);
    EXPECT_DOUBLE_EQ(my_module_compute(10.0, 5.0), 15.0);
    EXPECT_DOUBLE_EQ(my_module_compute(0.0, 0.0), 0.0);
}

TEST_F(MyModuleTest, DescribeConvertsStruct) {
    EXPECT_CALL(mock, describe(testing::_))
        .WillOnce([](const Point& p) {
            return "(" + std::to_string(p.x) + ", " + std::to_string(p.y) + ")";
        });

    Point p{ .x = 1.5, .y = 2.5 };
    // C bridge passes struct as void* — the bridge unwraps it
    const char* result = my_module_describe(&p);
    EXPECT_STREQ(result, "(1.500000, 2.500000)");
    free((void*)result);  // bridge calls strdup; test owns the result
}

TEST_F(MyModuleTest, EnumReturnIsConvertedToInt64) {
    EXPECT_CALL(mock, getStatus())
        .WillOnce(testing::Return(Status::RUNNING));

    int64_t status = my_module_get_status();
    EXPECT_EQ(status, static_cast<int64_t>(Status::RUNNING));
}
```

---

## CMake integration

```cmake
# tests/CMakeLists.txt
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.14)
project(my_module_tests)

add_executable(my_module_tests
    my_module_test.cpp
    "${GENERATED_CPP}/my_module.bridge.g.cpp"
    "${GENERATED_CPP}/test/my_module.test.g.cpp"
    "${SRC}/dart_api_dl.c"
)

target_include_directories(my_module_tests PRIVATE
    "${GENERATED_CPP}"
    "${GENERATED_CPP}/test"
    "${NITRO_NATIVE}"
)

target_link_libraries(my_module_tests
    gtest_main gmock_main
)

# C++17 required by Nitro-generated code
set_property(TARGET my_module_tests PROPERTY CXX_STANDARD 17)

include(GoogleTest)
gtest_discover_tests(my_module_tests)
```

---

## Testing error paths

The C bridge captures C++ exceptions via `nitro_report_error`. Test them:

```cpp
TEST_F(MyModuleTest, ExceptionBecomesNitroError) {
    EXPECT_CALL(mock, compute(testing::_, testing::_))
        .WillOnce(testing::Throw(std::runtime_error("overflow")));

    double result = my_module_compute(1e308, 1e308);
    // Bridge returns default (0.0) and sets the error slot
    EXPECT_DOUBLE_EQ(result, 0.0);

    NitroError* err = my_module_get_error();
    EXPECT_TRUE(err->hasError);
    EXPECT_STREQ(err->name, "CppException");
    EXPECT_NE(std::string(err->message).find("overflow"), std::string::npos);
    my_module_clear_error();
}
```

---

## Testing stream emission

```cpp
TEST_F(MyModuleTest, EmitStatusChangedPostsToDartPort) {
    // Register a fake Dart port using a callback mock
    int64_t captured_value = -1;

    // Implement a minimal Dart_CObject receiver
    my_module_register_on_status_changed_stream(0xDEAD);  // fake port

    // Trigger emission from native
    mock.emit_onStatusChanged(Status::DONE);

    // In real integration tests you'd verify via Dart_PostCObject_DL.
    // In unit tests, verify the port was set correctly.
    // (Full port integration requires a live Dart VM.)
}
```

---

## Running tests

```bash
cd build/
cmake ..
cmake --build . --target my_module_tests
ctest --output-on-failure
```

Or with `ninja`:
```bash
cmake -G Ninja ..
ninja my_module_tests
./my_module_tests
```

---

## `.clangd` IDE support

`nitrogen link` adds the generated test directory to `.clangd`:

```yaml
# .clangd (generated)
CompileFlags:
  Add:
    - "-I${workspaceFolder}/lib/src/generated/cpp"
    - "-I${workspaceFolder}/lib/src/generated/cpp/test"
    - "-I${workspaceFolder}/src/native"
```

This gives full IntelliSense for the mock and bridge headers in VS Code and CLion.
